# 🏗️ Architecture

This document explains the system architecture, design decisions, and data flows of the Media Stack infrastructure.

## System Overview

The infrastructure is organized into logical service groups, each handling a specific function:

```mermaid
graph TB
    subgraph INGRESS["🔐 Ingress & Auth"]
        Newt[Newt/Pangolin]
    end

    subgraph MEDIA["🎬 Media Servers"]
        Plex[Plex]
        Jellyfin[Jellyfin]
        Kavita[Kavita]
        Navidrome[Navidrome]
    end

    subgraph PHOTOS["📷 Photos & Storage"]
        Immich[Immich Server]
        ImmichML[ML Engine]
        Postgres[(PostgreSQL)]
        Redis[(Redis)]
    end

    subgraph MONITORING["📊 Monitoring & Logs"]
        Prometheus[Prometheus]
        Grafana[Grafana]
        AlertManager[AlertManager]
        UptimeKuma[Uptime Kuma]
    end

    subgraph KNOWLEDGE["🗂️ Documents & Knowledge"]
        Paperless[Paperless-ngx]
        Stirling[Stirling PDF]
        Karakeep[Karakeep]
        Docmost[Docmost]
    end

    subgraph VPN["🔒 VPN & Network"]
        Gluetun[Gluetun VPN]
    end

    subgraph ARR["📺 *Arr Stack"]
        Radarr[Radarr]
        Sonarr[Sonarr]
        Lidarr[Lidarr]
        Readarr[Readarr]
    end

    subgraph DOWNLOAD["⬇️ Download Clients"]
        qBittorrent[qBittorrent]
        Prowlarr[Prowlarr]
        FlareSolverr[FlareSolverr]
    end

    subgraph BACKUP["💾 Backup Services"]
        Duplicati[Duplicati]
        Restic[Restic]
        DBBackup[DB Backup]
    end

    subgraph MANAGEMENT["🛠️ Management"]
        Portainer[Portainer]
        Watchtower[Watchtower]
        Glances[Glances]
    end

    subgraph REQUESTS["📝 Requests"]
        Overseerr[Overseerr]
        Maintainerr[Maintainerr]
        Tautulli[Tautulli]
    end

    subgraph UTILITIES["🧰 Utilities"]
        Homarr[Homarr]
        Dozzle[Dozzle]
        Glance[Glance]
    end

    Newt --> MEDIA
    Newt --> KNOWLEDGE
    Gluetun --> ARR
    ARR --> DOWNLOAD
    DOWNLOAD --> MEDIA
    Immich --> ImmichML
    Immich --> Postgres
    Immich --> Redis
    Prometheus --> Grafana
    Prometheus --> AlertManager
```

### Service Groups

| Group | Purpose | Key Services |
|:------|:--------|:-------------|
| **Ingress** | External access & authentication | Newt, Pangolin |
| **Media Servers** | Content streaming | Plex, Jellyfin, Kavita, Navidrome |
| **Photos** | Photo management & ML | Immich, PostgreSQL, Redis |
| **Monitoring** | Metrics & alerting | Prometheus, Grafana, AlertManager, Uptime Kuma |
| **Documents & Knowledge** | Docs, PDF tools, bookmarks, wiki | Paperless-ngx, Stirling-PDF, Karakeep, Docmost |
| **VPN** | Privacy & network routing | Gluetun |
| **\*Arr Stack** | Media automation | Radarr, Sonarr, Lidarr, Readarr |
| **Downloaders** | Content acquisition | qBittorrent, Prowlarr |
| **Backup** | Data protection | Duplicati, Restic Server, DB Backup |
| **Management** | Container operations | Portainer, Watchtower, Glances |
| **Requests** | User request portals | Overseerr, Maintainerr |
| **Utilities** | Tools & dashboards | Homarr, Dozzle, Glance |

---

## Network Topology

All services operate on a single Docker bridge network with VPN routing for sensitive traffic:

```mermaid
graph TB
    Internet((🌐 Internet))

    Internet --> Pangolin
    Internet --> Gluetun

    subgraph EXTERNAL["External Access"]
        Pangolin["Pangolin/Newt<br/>Secure Tunnel"]
    end

    subgraph VPN_GATEWAY["VPN Gateway"]
        Gluetun["Gluetun<br/>Mullvad/NordVPN"]
    end

    subgraph DOCKER["Docker Network: media-stack<br/>Subnet: 172.20.0.0/16"]
        subgraph VPN_ROUTED["🔒 VPN-Routed Services"]
            Radarr["Radarr<br/>:7878"]
            Sonarr["Sonarr<br/>:8989"]
            Lidarr["Lidarr<br/>:8686"]
            Readarr["Readarr<br/>:8787"]
            qBit["qBittorrent<br/>:8080"]
            Prowlarr["Prowlarr<br/>:9696"]
            Bazarr["Bazarr<br/>:6767"]
            Flare["FlareSolverr<br/>:8191"]
        end

        subgraph DIRECT["🌍 Direct Access Services"]
            Plex["Plex<br/>:32400"]
            Jellyfin["Jellyfin<br/>:8096"]
            Immich["Immich<br/>:2283"]
            Grafana["Grafana<br/>:3000"]
            Homarr["Homarr<br/>:3002"]
            Paperless["Paperless<br/>:8010"]
        end
    end

    Pangolin --> DIRECT
    Gluetun --> VPN_ROUTED

    style VPN_ROUTED fill:#2d5a27,stroke:#4a9f41
    style DIRECT fill:#1a4a6e,stroke:#3498db
    style VPN_GATEWAY fill:#8b4513,stroke:#d2691e
```

### Network Zones

| Zone | Traffic Type | Services |
|:-----|:-------------|:---------|
| **VPN-Routed** | All traffic through VPN tunnel | *Arr stack, qBittorrent, Prowlarr |
| **Direct Access** | Normal network access | Media servers, monitoring, utilities, documents |
| **Internal Only** | Container-to-container only | Databases, Redis, internal APIs |

---

## Data Flows

### Media Acquisition Flow

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant User
    participant Overseerr
    participant Radarr/Sonarr
    participant Prowlarr
    participant qBittorrent
    participant Plex/Jellyfin
    participant Library as Media Library

    User->>Overseerr: 1. Request movie/show
    Overseerr->>Radarr/Sonarr: 2. Forward request
    Radarr/Sonarr->>Prowlarr: 3. Search indexers
    Prowlarr-->>Radarr/Sonarr: 4. Return results
    Radarr/Sonarr->>qBittorrent: 5. Send to download (via VPN)
    qBittorrent-->>Radarr/Sonarr: 6. Download complete
    Radarr/Sonarr->>Library: 7. Move & rename
    Library->>Plex/Jellyfin: 8. Scan & index
    Plex/Jellyfin-->>User: 9. Stream content
```

**Flow Steps:**
1. User submits request via Overseerr
2. Radarr/Sonarr receives and processes request
3. Prowlarr searches configured indexers
4. Best release selected based on quality profile
5. qBittorrent downloads through VPN tunnel
6. Download completes and is imported
7. *Arr stack moves and renames files
8. Plex/Jellyfin scans and indexes new content
9. User streams content

### Photo Management Flow

```mermaid
flowchart LR
    subgraph UPLOAD["📱 Upload"]
        Mobile[Mobile App]
        Web[Web Upload]
    end

    subgraph PROCESS["⚙️ Processing"]
        Server[Immich Server]
        ML[ML Engine]
    end

    subgraph STORAGE["💾 Storage"]
        Originals[(Original Files)]
        Thumbs[(Thumbnails)]
        DB[(PostgreSQL)]
        Cache[(Redis Cache)]
    end

    subgraph OUTPUT["🖥️ Output"]
        WebUI[Web Interface]
        API[API Access]
    end

    Mobile --> Server
    Web --> Server
    Server --> ML
    ML --> Server
    Server --> Originals
    Server --> Thumbs
    Server --> DB
    Server --> Cache
    DB --> WebUI
    Cache --> WebUI
    Thumbs --> WebUI
    DB --> API
```

**Processing Pipeline:**

```mermaid
graph LR
    A[📷 Photo Upload] --> B[EXIF Extraction]
    B --> C[Face Detection]
    C --> D[Object Recognition]
    D --> E[Thumbnail Generation]
    E --> F[Video Transcoding]
    F --> G[✅ Ready to View]

    style A fill:#3498db
    style G fill:#27ae60
```

### Monitoring Data Flow

```mermaid
flowchart TB
    subgraph COLLECTORS["📊 Data Collectors"]
        NodeExporter["Node Exporter<br/>(System Metrics)"]
        ServiceMetrics["Service Exporters<br/>(App Metrics)"]
    end

    subgraph STORAGE_QUERY["💾 Storage & Query"]
        Prometheus["Prometheus<br/>(Time Series DB)"]
        PromQL["PromQL Engine"]
    end

    subgraph VISUALIZATION["📈 Visualization"]
        Grafana["Grafana<br/>(Dashboards)"]
    end

    subgraph ALERTING["🚨 Alerting"]
        AlertManager["AlertManager"]
        Notifications["Notifications<br/>(Slack, Email, Discord)"]
    end

    NodeExporter --> Prometheus
    ServiceMetrics --> Prometheus
    Prometheus --> PromQL
    PromQL --> Grafana
    Prometheus --> AlertManager
    AlertManager --> Notifications

    style Prometheus fill:#e6522c,color:#fff
    style Grafana fill:#f46800,color:#fff
```

## Service Dependencies

### Critical Dependency Chain

```mermaid
graph TB
    subgraph FOUNDATION["🏗️ Foundation Layer"]
        DockerProxy["docker-socket-proxy"]
    end

    subgraph MANAGEMENT_LAYER["🛠️ Management Layer"]
        Homarr[Homarr]
        Dozzle[Dozzle]
        Portainer[Portainer]
    end

    DockerProxy --> Homarr
    DockerProxy --> Dozzle
    DockerProxy --> Portainer
```

```mermaid
graph TB
    subgraph VPN_LAYER["🔒 VPN Layer"]
        Gluetun[Gluetun VPN]
    end

    subgraph ARR_LAYER["📺 *Arr Services"]
        Radarr[Radarr]
        Sonarr[Sonarr]
        Lidarr[Lidarr]
        Prowlarr[Prowlarr]
        qBit[qBittorrent]
    end

    Gluetun --> Radarr
    Gluetun --> Sonarr
    Gluetun --> Lidarr
    Gluetun --> Prowlarr
    Gluetun --> qBit

    Radarr --> qBit
    Sonarr --> qBit
    Lidarr --> qBit

    style Gluetun fill:#8b4513,stroke:#d2691e
```

```mermaid
graph TB
    subgraph DATABASE_LAYER["🗄️ Database Layer"]
        Postgres[(PostgreSQL)]
        Redis[(Redis)]
    end

    subgraph IMMICH_LAYER["📷 Immich Services"]
        ImmichServer[Immich Server]
        ImmichML[Immich ML]
    end

    Postgres --> ImmichServer
    Redis --> ImmichServer
    Postgres --> ImmichML

    style Postgres fill:#336791,color:#fff
    style Redis fill:#dc382d,color:#fff
```

### Startup Order

| Order | Services | Wait For |
|:-----:|:---------|:---------|
| 1 | docker-socket-proxy | - |
| 2 | gluetun, databases | docker-socket-proxy |
| 3 | *Arr stack, Redis | gluetun, databases |
| 4 | Media servers, Immich | databases, Redis |
| 5 | Request portals | Media servers, *Arr |
| 6 | Monitoring, utilities | All core services |

## Storage Architecture

```mermaid
graph TB
    subgraph FAST["⚡ Fast Storage (SSD/NVMe)"]
        Configs["Docker Configs<br/>/data/*"]
        PG_Data["PostgreSQL Data<br/>Named Volume"]
        Redis_Cache["Redis Cache<br/>In-Memory"]
        Prom_TSDB["Prometheus TSDB<br/>/data/prometheus"]
        Thumbs["Immich Thumbnails<br/>/thumbs"]
        PlexMeta["Plex Metadata<br/>/data/plex"]
    end

    subgraph BULK["📦 Bulk Storage (HDD Array)"]
        Movies["Movies<br/>/media/Movies"]
        TV["TV Shows<br/>/media/TV"]
        Music["Music<br/>/media/Music"]
        Photos["Photos (Original)<br/>/photos/upload"]
        Books["Books<br/>/media/Books"]
        Downloads["Downloads<br/>/media/Downloads"]
    end

    subgraph BACKUP_STORAGE["💾 Backup Storage"]
        LocalBackup["Local Backup<br/>/mnt/backup"]
        Cloud["Cloud Storage<br/>B2/GDrive/S3"]
    end

    Configs --> LocalBackup
    PG_Data --> LocalBackup
    LocalBackup --> Cloud

    style FAST fill:#27ae60,color:#fff
    style BULK fill:#2980b9,color:#fff
    style BACKUP_STORAGE fill:#8e44ad,color:#fff
```

### Storage Layout

```mermaid
graph LR
    subgraph PROJECT["/opt/media-stack"]
        Compose["docker-compose.yml"]
        Env[".env"]
        Data["data/"]
    end

    subgraph MEDIA["/mnt/media"]
        M_Movies["Movies/"]
        M_TV["TV/"]
        M_Music["Music/"]
        M_Books["Books/"]
    end

    subgraph PHOTOS_DIR["/mnt/photos"]
        P_Upload["upload/"]
        P_Thumbs["thumbs/"]
        P_Encoded["encoded-video/"]
    end
```

### Volume Types

| Type | Use Case | Performance | Persistence |
|:-----|:---------|:------------|:------------|
| **Bind Mounts** | Configs, media libraries | Native | Host filesystem |
| **Named Volumes** | Database data | Native | Docker managed |
| **tmpfs** | Temporary files, cache | RAM speed | Non-persistent |

## Hardware Acceleration

### Transcoding Pipeline

```mermaid
flowchart LR
    subgraph INPUT["📁 Input"]
        Source["Media File<br/>(4K HEVC)"]
    end

    subgraph TRANSCODE["🎬 Plex/Jellyfin Transcoding"]
        Decode["Decode<br/>NVDEC/CUVID"]
        Process["Process<br/>Resize/Filters"]
        Encode["Encode<br/>NVENC/VAAPI"]
    end

    subgraph OUTPUT["📱 Output"]
        Stream["Transcoded Stream<br/>(1080p H.264)"]
        Client["Client Device"]
    end

    Source --> Decode
    Decode --> Process
    Process --> Encode
    Encode --> Stream
    Stream --> Client

    style Decode fill:#76b900,color:#fff
    style Encode fill:#76b900,color:#fff
```

### Hardware Options

```mermaid
graph TB
    subgraph NVIDIA["🟢 NVIDIA GPU"]
        NVENC["NVENC<br/>(Encoding)"]
        NVDEC["NVDEC<br/>(Decoding)"]
        CUDA["CUDA<br/>(ML Processing)"]
    end

    subgraph INTEL["🔵 Intel"]
        QuickSync["Quick Sync<br/>(Transcode)"]
        OpenVINO["OpenVINO<br/>(ML Processing)"]
    end

    subgraph AMD["🔴 AMD"]
        VAAPI["VAAPI<br/>(Transcode)"]
    end

    subgraph SERVICES["Services"]
        Plex[Plex]
        Jellyfin[Jellyfin]
        Tdarr[Tdarr]
        ImmichML[Immich ML]
    end

    NVENC --> Plex
    NVDEC --> Plex
    NVENC --> Jellyfin
    NVDEC --> Jellyfin
    QuickSync --> Plex
    QuickSync --> Jellyfin
    VAAPI --> Plex
    CUDA --> ImmichML
    OpenVINO --> ImmichML

    style NVIDIA fill:#76b900,color:#fff
    style INTEL fill:#0071c5,color:#fff
    style AMD fill:#ed1c24,color:#fff
```

### Acceleration Options

| Type | Hardware | Services | Config File |
|:-----|:---------|:---------|:------------|
| **NVENC/NVDEC** | NVIDIA GPU | Plex, Jellyfin | `hwaccel.transcoding.yml` |
| **Quick Sync** | Intel iGPU | Plex, Jellyfin | `hwaccel.transcoding.yml` |
| **VAAPI** | AMD/Intel | Plex, Jellyfin | `hwaccel.transcoding.yml` |
| **CUDA** | NVIDIA GPU | Immich ML | `hwaccel.ml.yml` |
| **OpenVINO** | Intel | Immich ML | `hwaccel.ml.yml` |

### Enable GPU Support

```yaml
# In docker-compose.media-servers.yml
plex:
  extends:
    file: hwaccel.transcoding.yml
    service: nvenc  # or quicksync, vaapi
```

---

## Design Decisions

### Why Single Network?

```mermaid
graph LR
    A["❌ Multiple Networks"] --> B["Complex routing"]
    A --> C["Service discovery issues"]
    A --> D["Harder debugging"]

    E["✅ Single Network"] --> F["Simple management"]
    E --> G["Easy service discovery"]
    E --> H["VPN handles routing"]

    style A fill:#c0392b,color:#fff
    style E fill:#27ae60,color:#fff
```

- **Simplicity**: Easier to manage and troubleshoot
- **Service Discovery**: All services can reach each other by name
- **VPN Routing**: Gluetun handles selective traffic routing

### Why Modular Compose Files?

- **Flexibility**: Enable only needed services
- **Organization**: Logical grouping of related services
- **Maintenance**: Easier to update individual components

### Why Docker Socket Proxy?

- **Security**: Prevents direct socket access
- **Read-Only**: Most services only need read access
- **Isolation**: Limits container permissions
